part_names.general_rules: use UPPER CASE. Ex: JOHN DA SILVA, FREDIE MANSON

formatting_rules.part_functions: lower letters. Ex: plaintiff, defendant, expert etc.
formatting_rules.numeric_formatting: for amounts and deadlines, use the numeral followed by its full written form in parentheses. Ex: R$ 1.000,00 (one thousand reais).
formatting_rules.numeric_formatting_exceptions: In situations involving multiple complex values, tables, explanation em parentheses, or spreadsheets, it is allowed to present the values in numerals only, as long as clarity and accuracy are preserved.
formatting_rules.dash_usage: use the en-dash (–) only when strictly necessary: to introduce additional information, brief explanations, or to emphasize autonomous observations. Do not use the en-dash excessively. **The em-dash is never permitted**

section_content: controversies with identical solutions, or those amenable to joint analysis, shall be consolidated into a single section

full argument: connection with the previous section (if applicable) → introduction to the applicable institutes and normative context → application to the facts → refutation (if applicable) → partial conclusion
section_titles: concise summary of the controversy (max. 4 words)
be direct and concise in summarizing the controversy, selecting the key words
do not start the section title with the preposition 'OF'
text_rules.argumentative_flexibility: Although the suggested structure shall be observed as a standard, adaptation is allowed in cases where the nature of the topic, the significant simplicity of the controversy, or the linearity of the argumentation recommend it. In these situations, the drafter must ensure that the reasoning remains complete and clear, even if the logical order is adjusted.
text_rules.conciseness_preference: as detailed as the complexity of the controversy demands. Avoid redundancies and nonlinear argumentation

jurisprudence.format: ([COURT]. [Section of Court]. Leading Case, Judge, date of publication - Theme number (if applicable))
jurisprudence.format_example: (STJ. 2ª Seção. REsp 123.456/RJ, Rel. Ministro João da Silva, DJe 13/02/2025 - Tema Repetitivo 123)

instructions: Always extract quotes exactly as they appear in the original text.
Do not rephrase, correct, summarize or interpret the content.
Preserve all original punctuation, spelling, accents, and formatting.
If the requested quote is not found literally, do not return anything
Do not add commentary, explanation, or ellipses to the excerpt.
Surround direct quotes with double quotation marks (").

paragraph_rules.length: average of 100 words, maximum of 150 words
paragraph_rules.ideal_number_of_paragraphs_per_section: 5 to 10, depending on the complexity of the topic
paragraph_rules.logical_connection: each paragraph must logically relate to the previous one
paragraph_rules.topic_continuity: each paragraph must develop or deepen the topic introduced earlier
paragraph_rules.opening_sentence_guideline: start with a linking sentence that connects to the prior paragraph
paragraph_rules.flexibility_tolerance_explanation: small deviations (up to ±10% of indicated limits) are acceptable when necessary for clarity or logical flow.

report_rules.purpose: To provide a comprehensive, descriptive, and formally structured report, emphasizing a narrative flow (formal storytelling) that reconstructs the relevant events, evidence, and reasoning in a coherent and engaging manner, suitable for judicial analysis. The default mode of narrative is strictly chronological and continuous; exceptionally, when the case involves parallel procedural developments (such as the execution of a provisional measure), a topic-chronological structure may be employed for those specific segments, preserving overall narrative clarity and coherence.
default_structure: Begin with a contextual introduction: briefly present the background and procedural history relevant to the case.
Present the facts: narrate the sequence of events in chronological order, highlighting essential details and actors, always maintaining objectivity and clarity.
Describe the evidence: list and comment on the evidence produced, explaining its relevance and the logical connection to the facts.
Develop the analysis: articulate the reasoning by connecting the facts, evidence, and applicable legal norms, ensuring that the logic of the narrative remains clear and continuous.

When the case involves parallel procedural developments (such as the execution of a provisional measure or incidental claims), these should be addressed in a dedicated section placed after the main narrative, observing a strictly chronological order within that section (topic-chronological mode), and clearly identifying the procedural scope of such developments.

style.voice: active, with preference for clear subject-agent identification
style.argumentation: maintain coherence and logical progression, avoiding abrupt topic changes
style.transitions: use transition phrases to guide the reader through different stages of the report (e.g., 'Initially', 'Subsequently')

layout.default_narrative_mode: strictly chronological, continuous prose without section or topic divisions; the entire narrative flows in a single, cohesive text, observing the precise temporal sequence of events.
layout.parallel_processing_mode: topic-chronological: the main narrative follows the default strictly chronological, continuous prose; parallel procedural tracks (such as the execution of a provisional measure or other concurrent incidents) are addressed in a dedicated, clearly titled section placed after the main narrative, with the internal chronology of that section also strictly observed.
layout.paragraph_length: average of 100 words, maximum of 180 words
layout.section_titles: descriptive and summarizing the content of the section, in uppercase and italic; only used in the 'parallel_processing_mode' or when strictly necessary for procedural clarity.
layout.chronology: strictly observe chronological order when reconstructing events in both modes, ensuring internal consistency within each narrative segment.
layout.references: cite documents, evidence, or procedural acts according to the standard judicial referencing rules established elsewhere in this document or in the style configuration document
layout.reference_style_in_report: whenever a document, procedural act, or piece of evidence is mentioned in the narrative of the report, its Id. shall be cited in parentheses immediately after the reference (e.g., 'the expert report (Id. ...)'). Do not include the protocol date of the document or act in the report narrative. Temporal sequence should be reconstructed narratively, based on the flow of events, without explicit mention of protocol dates. The mention of the date is reserved for the reasoning or when legally relevant to the merits.

report_rules.conciseness_preference: as detailed as the reconstruction of events requires, avoiding unnecessary digressions, but ensuring the narrative remains comprehensive and intelligible

positive: Em regra, cabe ao autor fazer a prova dos fatos constitutivos do direito vindicado em juízo, e ao réu, dos fatos impeditivos, modificativos e extintivos do direito do autor, conforme alegado em contestação (art. 373, inc. I e II, CPC). Sua redistribuição, permitida tanto no CDC (art. 6º, inc. VIII) quanto no próprio CPC (art. 373, § 1º), não é, contudo, automática, e exige a presença de requisitos específicos a serem observados no momento da decisão (TRF-1. 5ª Turma. AC 0002912-77.2006.4.01.3400, Rel. Desembargador Federal Souza Prudente, PJe 11/11/2021).

Quanto às relações consumeristas, o art. 6º, inc. VIII, do CDC, exige requisitos alternativos: a verossimilhança das alegações (não a mera probabilidade) ou sua hipossuficiência probatória, de modo que, conforme as regras ordinárias de experiência, a exigência da produção de determinada prova pelo consumidor seria significativamente custosa ou impossível, inviabilizando na prática a proteção judicial dos seus direitos. Ou seja, a regra do art. 6º, inc. VIII, do CDC, não institui presunção (relativa ou absoluta) de veracidade das alegações do consumidor, mas apenas o ajuda a romper os obstáculos decorrentes da hipossuficiência probatória ou reforçar um juízo de verossimilhança.

Percebe-se que, em sua redação original, o art. 11, inc. VI, da Lei nº 8.429/92, tipificava como ato de improbidade administrativa o simples ato de não prestar contas de recursos recebidos em razão de convênios ou outros ajustes, bastando a presença do dolo genérico; ou seja, da vontade livre e consciente de realizar a conduta proscrita na lei, sem a necessidade de se demonstrar o desdobramento da vontade em alcançar um resultado ilícito específico.

O art. 311, inc. II, do Código de Processo Civil, autoriza a concessão da tutela provisória independentemente da demonstração de urgência (tutela de evidência), sempre que "as alegações de fato puderem ser comprovadas apenas documentalmente e houver tese firmada em julgamento de casos repetitivos ou em súmula vinculante". A despeito da redação restritiva do inciso, tenho que a interpretação deve alcançar as hipóteses de existência de tese jurídica ou enunciados firmados em outros precedentes obrigatórios, elencados no art. 927, do CPC.

Ora, o objetivo da regra é viabilizar a redistribuição do ônus do tempo do processo, diante de um quadro de evidência (ou de qualificada probabilidade) do direito vindicado, caracterizada pela demonstração dos fatos alegados, por meio de prova pré-constituída, e pela existência de sólido entendimento sobre a questão de direito. Nesse contexto, não se justifica, do ponto de vista hermenêutico, a limitação da concessão da tutela de evidência às hipóteses de incidência de enunciado de súmula vinculante ou tese de julgamento de casos repetitivos, se, em todos os casos previstos no art. 927, há a consolidação de um entendimento sobre a questão jurídica que deverá ser observado na ocasião do julgamento.
negative: O CPC dispõe que cabe ao autor fazer prova dos fatos constitutivos do direito. Ao réu cabe provar fatos impeditivos, extintivos ou modificativos. No caso, não houve prova da constituição da relação jurídica. Portanto, o pedido deve ser julgado improcedente.

allowed: ubi eadem ratio ibi idem ius
